hypothesis: Integrating a specialized molecular image detection and segmentation pre-processing module—trained specifically to localize and isolate chemical structure depictions within heterogeneous scientific article layouts—into the single-agent extraction pipeline will yield a statistically significant improvement in SMILES extraction F1 scores (from near-zero to above 0.40) across both the nanomaterials and small molecule datasets in ChemX, demonstrating that the primary bottleneck for chemical structure extraction is image localization rather than structure-to-SMILES conversion capability of the underlying language model.
